Title: The Label
Label: shuNai Echoes
Album Description:
Virtue's Paradox is more than an album; it is a Surgical Audit performed on the nerves of identity. As the definitive manifesto of Acoustic Brutalism, this 14-track record is built from the debris of systemic illusions and personal collapse, moving act-by-act through a deliberate five-part descent.

The Sonic Narrative:
The record transitions from the atmospheric vacuum of Acoustic Noir (The Last Petal) into the "Chapters of Decay" (Wolf and Jackal, VALUE$), documenting the disintegration of internal empires through Industrial Rock and Brutal Bap. The journey evolves through Glitch-Folk and nostalgic Retrospective Roots (Secret Notebook) before reaching the "quiet-loud" zenith of wreckage and final catharsis in the Dark Carnival of "Curtain Call".

The Technical Manifesto:
Driven by the principle of Biological Integrity, the album rejects the sterile perfection of modern production. Every track adheres to the RHA Protocol:

No Pitch Correction: Preserving the natural rasp, vocal fry, and raw emotional breath of the baritone delivery.

Unquantized Dynamics: Utilizing live, "stumbling" rhythms and aggressive industrial textures to treat audio as physical architecture.

High-Contrast Density: Achieving "heaviness" not through volume, but through the uncompromising density of human truth.

Virtue's Paradox offers a raw, cinematic frame for the disillusioned. It is the sound of a consciousness choosing its own fate amidst the digital dissonance of 2026.

The track features a sharp, sarcastic male vocal that shifts from weary, rhythmic verses to explosive, anthemic grunge shouts in the chorus. Lyrics:
Midnight calls, your voice, a widdle bunny's plea
Sweet nothings whispered, meant for only me
The world outside, a blur, a chaotic, passing show
Lost in your digital glow, nowhere else to go

Moved in with just a suitcase, a pawn on your grand board
Believing in a future, a love I could afford
Tasted life drawn from your very soul
Traced your every contour, losing all control

Then many years dissolved like sugar, you sculpted me anew
From a nameless cipher, to someone shining through
A living testament, a trophy you could claim
While I played the part you wrote, fueling your ambition's flame

But oh, the irony, the joke that time will play
Those burning years, those passions, just started to decay!
The vibrant hues all faded, the deep truths turned to rust
Leaving just a label, turning memories to dust!

You asked why months went silent, no spark, no touch, no sign?
I sought cheap solace elsewhere, a different, tangled line
While you looked past the shadows, a narrative you spun
And when you walked away, my very core felt undone!

But oh, the irony, the joke that time will play
Those burning years, those passions, just started to decay!
The vibrant hues all faded, the deep truths turned to rust
Leaving just a label, turning memories to dust!

Came back, then left again, the cycle spun too fast
A year of bitter whispers, futures built to never last
Now decades fade behind us, the vivid colors cease
Just a twisted grin when I see your hard-won, hollow peace
You married "the gentle touch," you sneered, who never fought the fray
While I stand rough and bearded, the strength you threw away
Now he cleans and cooks and whispers, trapped in her command
While she, who craved raw power, got a tightly controlled hand

And oh, the irony, the joke that time will play
Those burning years, those passions, just started to decay!
The vibrant hues all faded, the deep truths turned to rust
Leaving just a label, turning memories to dust!

Then lightning strikes the mind, a question, crude and deep
A memory replaced, secrets I couldn't keep
Did she swallow? Or spit it out? That thought, it makes me reel
So many years we lived as one and all that's left is the
Feel?!

I still look you in the eye
Even when the world says look away

They crumble like paper when the pressure gets loud
Nodding their heads before the question is out

I don't look away from the man in the glass

Last samurai staring down an endless host
Vikings charging straight into the fire and the end
Smiling at death like they're meeting an old friend
You traded your honor just to stay alive one more day
We kept the flame — even if it burns us away
You sold your soul for the comfort of the crowd
We stayed human and that's why we stand proud

Honor doesn't die while one man still stands
Saying "no" with his eyes as the kingdom turns to sand
I seek no reward, I chase no borrowed fame
This is the last honest thing I refuse to betray
I'm the last samurai who remembers the code
And my "no" is the line where the river won't erode

So look me in the eye
I won't look away
Even if I'm the last one standing here today

Lyrics:
That distant year, the world felt new,
I spoke in riddles, hid the real me from you.
A fragile mask, "empty girl," you see,
While coded whispers danced inside of me.

That driving beat, a rebellious sound,
My paper shield, spinning round and round.
I played my game, a dangerous, sweet art,
Hiding the truth, tearing worlds apart…

Then the island's heavy air, thick and gray,
That song stole my choice, turned my world astray.
"I don't wanna miss a thing", the speakers cried,
Choice... astray...
But I had to choose, where a piece of me died.
A future veiled, a choice that cut so deep,
For virtue's name, so much to keep.
A 'normo' mother? Not in this land.
Just Ang Mo's ghost, beyond your command.

They call me Virtue's Void, "virtuous" they say,
But when life feels right, I break the day.
You offer kindness, comfort, gold,
Kindness offered...
And I build walls, a story told
Of breaking things, of pushing hard,
Pushing hard... destroyed...
Leaving every promise scarred.
"Don't interfere," I always plead,
Your bitter seed... "It's my own fault."
Was it the fear? Was it fear?
Or just the echo, year after year?

Years drift like smoke, you reappear,
A bright, kind ghost, banishing my fear.
Your fear... banished...
You paid my debts, brought diamond light,
While I just drifted, day and night.
Then 'Lah,' so what lah?' this life I led,
Life led... words unsaid...
With other men… the words unsaid.
You saw my fragile hope, my guiding star,
Guiding star... afar...
While real life played out, oh so sad.
It needs protection, safe and true,
Needs to be true...
But Virtue's Void… what can she do?

They call me Virtue's Void, "virtuous" they say,
But when life feels right, I break the day.
You offer kindness, comfort, gold,
Kindness offered...
And I build walls, a story told
Of breaking things, of pushing hard,
Pushing hard... destroyed...
Leaving every promise scarred.
"Don't interfere," I always plead,
Your bitter seed...
"It's my own fault." Was it the fear?
Was it fear?
Or just the echo, year after year?

Maybe the "Ang Mo" seed you see,
Is just the part that longs to be...
Anything but this control,
This need to shatter, lose my soul.
I protect the weak, I fight their fight,
But lose myself in endless night.

"My son..." whispered in the dark.
My son... so sad...
"My fault..." leaving its painful mark.
Your fault...
Virtue's Void... lost and gone.
Void... gone...
Her name... playing on...
Just Her name... fading...

Description: A deeply intimate, stripped-back remaster of the original acoustic single, reimagined as a raw, dark folk/symphonic cello-metal studio session. Captured in a dead room with dry studio acoustics and a close-up mic, the track strips away all venue resonance to focus on a warm, analog preamp feel with natural breathing and small human imperfections. I still look you in the eye
Even when the world says look away

Nodding their heads before the question is out

But silence feels cleaner than a borrowed "yes" tonight
I don't look away from the man in the glass
I just won't let him wear somebody else's mask

Every "yes" they choke down leaves another scar
I'd rather bleed honest than pretend who we are

Honor doesn't die while one man still stands
Saying "no" with his eyes and the weight of his hands
Not for glory, not for praise, not for anyone's crown
Just because bending would burn the last honest thing down

They take what isn't theirs like the world owes them rent
Cross every line like the ground was never meant
No real evil, no hatred, just blind and carefree
They drift through this life like it's all theirs for free
I could've stepped aside, let the moment slip away
But I chose the harder road — and kept my own ground to stay

Honor doesn't die while one man still stands
Saying "no" with his eyes when the whole world demands
Not for glory, not for praise, not for anyone's throne
Just to keep the last piece of myself I can still call my own

They call us fools who fight when the battle's already lost
Last samurai staring down an endless host
Vikings charging straight into the fire and the end
Smiling at death like they're meeting an old friend
You traded your honor just to stay alive one more day
We kept the flame — even if it burns us away
You sold your soul for the comfort of the crowd
We stayed human… and that's why we stand proud

Honor doesn't die while one man still stands
Saying "no" with his eyes as the kingdom turns to sand

And my "no" is the line where the river won't erode

So look me in the eye
I won't look away
Even if I'm the last one standing here today
